title: Wip/adam/ps 69090
author: AdamCollins

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Adds targeted integration/framework tests (and a coverage report artifact) around filesystem-backed behaviors in pictures, photos, drawings, and file library flows to improve exercised coverage of non–Trimble Connect paths and attachment/file creation.

**Changes:**
- Added multiple new MSTest integration tests validating on-disk output locations and missing-file recovery (pictures/photos/drawings).
- Introduced a new `DMSSystemTests` suite covering file gathering/zipping and related file-library operations.
- Added a generated-style Markdown coverage report for filesystem IO methods.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 8 out of 8 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| docs/temp/tfs-prep-non-tc-files/rw-test-coverage-report.md | Adds a filesystem IO method coverage report (Markdown). |
| Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/Records/Projects/ProjectPictureTests.cs | Adds assertion of expected disk path for saved project pictures. |
| Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/Records/Portfolios/OrganizationPictureTests.cs | Adds tests for org-picture missing-file recreation and expected disk path on save. |
| Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/Records/Drawings/DrawingsFromFMS.cs | Adds a non-TC-path drawing processing test exercising FMS-based flow. |
| Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/Photo Management/PhotoUploadTests.cs | Adds test ensuring photo processing from FMS results in a stored photo file on disk. |
| Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/Photo Management/PhotoCRUDTests.cs | Adds tests for photo file-link attachment population, missing-file behavior, and rotated-photo sync behavior. |
| Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/FileLibrary/DMSSystemTests.cs | New test class covering DMS file creation, zip gathering, link zipping, external executor upload, and annotated download creation. |
| Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelFrameworkTests/WebUI/APITests/DrawingSystemControllerTests.cs | Adds API test for fast-track validation on corrupt “PDF” upload content. |
</details>
reviews.author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 17 out of 17 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 22 out of 22 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.
reviews.author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]

review_comments.body: All of the Markdown tables in this report appear to start rows with `||` (double leading pipe). In GitHub-flavored Markdown this creates an extra empty first column, so the tables render misaligned. Consider doing a bulk replace so each row starts with a single `|` (e.g., `| Category | Count | % of total |`).
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Maybe a _slight nitpick_ for the 0 param, should we use a named variable or something like requestedBy.ProjectID (similar to how it's done in the project picture tests)?
review_comments.author: aamacavei

review_comments.body: This test deletes the FMS file for an arbitrary published spec (`publishedSpecs.First(s => s.FileID > 0)`), but then sends a Submittal Scribe request for `specSet.Specifications.First()`. In parallel runs, those can be different records, so the test may (a) delete another test’s file and/or (b) not actually exercise the missing-file path for the request being sent.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: This test picks the first published spec with a file from `GetSpecificationsNotLinkedToSubmittals()`, which can be nondeterministic (and in parallel runs can select a spec created by another test). That makes the test flaky and weakly coupled to its own setup; it should target the spec set created in this test.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: This test selects an arbitrary published spec (`FirstOrDefault(s => s.FileID > 0)`) instead of the spec created by `CreateNewSpecSetAndPublishAsync()`. In parallel integration runs, that can couple the test to other tests’ data and lead to flaky results; match on the spec created in this test (e.g., by `SpecificationID`).
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: The synchronous wrapper uses `Task.Run(...).Result`, which needlessly schedules work on the thread pool and wraps exceptions in `AggregateException`. Prefer calling the async method directly via `.GetAwaiter().GetResult()` for a synchronous bridge in test utilities.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Same issue as `FetchPhoto`: using `Task.Run(...).Result` adds thread-pool overhead and wraps exceptions. Use `.GetAwaiter().GetResult()` to synchronously wait on the existing async method.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: `ReadOne` no longer populates `TFSId`, so `FilePath` will always resolve to the FMS path even when a drawing/spec is stored in TFS. Also, `s_enc_get_Drawing_FileInfo` currently selects `d.TFSId` (see `Core/SQL/delta_procs.sql:117-125`), so removing the read is inconsistent with the stored proc output.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: `publishedSpecs.First(...)` will throw if no published spec matches the predicate (e.g., if the first spec in the set isn't the one with `FileID > 0`). This turns a legitimate "test setup" failure into an unhelpful exception and can make the test flaky across data variations.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: The sync wrappers use `Task.Run(...).Result`, which adds an unnecessary thread hop and can wrap exceptions in `AggregateException`. For a simple sync wrapper, block on the existing task with `GetAwaiter().GetResult()` (or remove the sync wrapper if it isn't needed).
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Same issue as `FetchPhoto(...)`: `Task.Run(...).Result` is an unnecessary thread hop and can wrap exceptions. Prefer `GetAwaiter().GetResult()` for a simple synchronous wrapper.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Markdown tables in this report use a double leading pipe (`||`), which renders as an extra empty first column in most Markdown renderers. Use a single leading pipe for each row (and keep the separator row aligned) so the table displays correctly.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: The synchronous wrappers use `Task.Run(...).Result`, which can mask exceptions (AggregateException) and adds unnecessary thread pool scheduling. Prefer `GetAwaiter().GetResult()` for a sync wrapper, or keep call sites async-only.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Same concern as `FetchPhoto(...)`: `Task.Run(...).Result` is an unnecessary blocking pattern for a sync wrapper and can wrap failures in `AggregateException`. Use `GetAwaiter().GetResult()` instead (or keep this API async-only).
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: `First(...)` will throw an `InvalidOperationException` (aborting the test) if the expected published spec isn't found. Using `FirstOrDefault` with an assertion yields a clearer failure message and keeps the test focused on the missing prerequisite.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Commenting out `TFSId` avoids a missing-column exception, but it also forces `TFSId` to remain `Guid.Empty` even if the stored procedure later starts returning the column. Prefer a conditional read that only populates `TFSId` when the column exists so TFS-backed files still work when available.
review_comments.author: Copilot

+# Filesystem IO Methods — Coverage Report
+
+**Sources:** `docs/temp/tfs-prep-non-tc-files/PS-69089-read-methods.md` · `docs/temp/tfs-prep-non-tc-files/PS-69093-write-methods.md`  
+**Coverage data:** `vs-coverage-files/` (5 daily-build runs, 2026-05-29)  
+**Aggregation:** max `line_coverage` across all 5 runs per method  
+**Thresholds:** Covered ≥ 40% · Partial 10–39% · Not Covered < 10%  
+**Dir column:** R = read-only · W = write-only · RW = appears in both lists
+
+---
+
+## Summary
+
+| Category | Count | % of total |
+|---|---|---|
+| **Covered** (≥ 40%) | 136 | 42.2% |
+| **Partial** (10–39%) | 8 | 2.5% |
+| **Not Covered** (< 10%) | 28 | 8.7% |
+| **Not in coverage data** | 150 | 46.6% |
+| **Total unique methods** | **322** | |
+
+**Breakdown by list membership:** 135 read-only · 81 write-only · 106 RW (in both lists)
+
+> **"Not in coverage data"** means the method's class was absent from all 5 coverage XML files — the assembly was either not instrumented or not loaded by these test runs (e.g. web-layer controllers, SDK projects, TC-migration pipelines).
+
